items.headline: Nigerian unions launch nationwide strike, shutting down the power grid.
items.body: Organized labor began an indefinite strike over the national minimum wage. This action immediately halted power and transport for millions of people.

items.headline: Argentina's Senate approves President Milei's sweeping state reform package.
items.body: The controversial bill passed after a tense and prolonged debate. The law now grants the executive broad new powers over the economy.

items.headline: Mexico's peso falls sharply after the ruling party's landslide election.
items.body: The currency dropped over four percent on fears of constitutional changes. This immediately increases the cost of imported goods for Mexican consumers.

items.headline: South Korea announces new cash payments to boost its birth rate.
items.body: The government is offering new financial incentives to parents. These policies aim to address the world's lowest fertility rate.

items.headline: US President Trump rejects Iran peace proposal on day 73 of conflict
items.body: Trump dismissed Iran's latest peace offer without explanation as oil hit $104 per barrel. American households have already lost $284 on average to higher fuel costs since the war began.

items.headline: Iran-linked group claims 17 arson attacks on Jewish sites across Europe
items.body: Synagogues and schools in London and other European cities were set ablaze by a shadowy group that emerged in March. CNN found links between the attackers and an Iran-backed Shia paramilitary organization.

items.headline: Indonesia volcano eruption kills three hikers including two Singaporeans found holding each other
items.body: Mount Dukono on Halmahera Island erupted Thursday trapping 20 climbers who defied safety restrictions. Rescuers recovered two bodies under rock debris near the crater rim.

items.headline: France and Kenya sign joint venture for $800 million port and logistics infrastructure
items.body: The agreement emerged from the Africa Forward Summit that closed Monday with bilateral deals across five nations. Kenya also secured French financing to rehabilitate its commuter rail network.
